about.heading: Virintira — more than an accounting firm, your trusted business partner.
paragraphs: With a team bringing over 10 years of experience, we provide end-to-end business support—from company registration, filings and permits, to monthly accounting, tax submissions, and structured growth planning.
So you can stay focused on growing your business with confidence, we take care of the back-office work—accurately and professionally.
At every step, we communicate clearly, keep everything transparent and verifiable, and operate in full compliance with Thai law.
With fast, attentive, and thorough service, we stand by you through every stage of growth—because “your success is our goal.”

requirements.title: What to prepare for company registration
requirements.subtitle: Simply prepare the basic information, and leave the rest to us.

documents.title: 1. Important documents required
items:
At least 2 shareholders 

1. Thai shareholders: Copy of ID card (Clear copy of front and back)

2. Foreign shareholders: Copy of passport (Only the first page with photo)

3. Corporate shareholders: Copy of company certificate and copy of authorized director's ID card
House registration to be used as the company's head office

information.title: 2. Basic business information
items: Registered capital amount (e.g., 1 million THB capital, 25% paid-up)

Company contact info 
1. Phone number 
2. Email

Shareholders' contact info 
1. Phone number 
2. Email 
3. LINE ID

steps.title: Consultation & Name Reservation
steps.description: Registering a company is like tailoring a suit. We discuss your needs, help plan the business structure that best fits your enterprise, and proceed to reserve the company name.

steps.title: Prepare documents via government system
steps.description: Our team prepares all documents and official forms via the DBD Biz Regist system of the Department of Business Development, Ministry of Commerce.

steps.title: Online Identity Verification (No travel required)
steps.description: Shareholders and directors verify their identity (Thai citizens use the ThaID app, foreigners use the DBD e-Service app) by scanning a QR Code or clicking a verification link sent by the DBD to their personal email.

steps.title: Submit Registration & Deliver
steps.description: Our team submits the application to the DBD. Once the registration and company certificate are approved, we will verify the accuracy and deliver the essential documents along with your free gifts directly to you.

items.question: What is the difference between a Company Limited (Co., Ltd.) and a Limited Partnership (Ltd. Part.), and which one should I choose?
items.answer: Although both structures have "Juristic Person" status and share the same tax rates, the main differences lie in "Liability for Debts", "Credibility", and "Restrictions on Foreigners". To make it easier to understand, here is a comparison: 1. Company Limited (Co., Ltd.) • Liability: "Limited Liability" (except for directors). All shareholders are liable for the company's debts only up to the unpaid amount of the shares they hold (clearly separates personal finances from business finances, securing personal assets). • Credibility: Very High. Suitable for long-term business, government bidding, large projects, attracting investors, or B2B (Business-to-Business) operations. • Foreigners / Work Permit: Highly Recommended. Laws and government agencies fully support the Co., Ltd. structure, whether it's for foreign shareholding (up to 49%), Work Permit applications, Business Visas, or BOI investment promotions. • Founders required: Currently, a minimum of only 2 shareholders is required to register. 2. Limited Partnership (Ltd. Part.) • Liability: The law requires at least one partner to act as the "Managing Partner", who has "Unlimited Liability" for the business's debts (if the business faces issues, the managing partner may have to use personal assets to pay off debts). Other partners have limited liability based on their investment. • Credibility: Moderate. Suitable for small businesses, Family Businesses, or enterprises where partners are not strict about the legal entity type. • Foreigners: Not Recommended. Having foreigners in a Ltd. Part. involves complex legal restrictions. If a foreigner acts as the managing partner, the business may be classified as a foreign entity, making operations and Work Permits much more difficult than a Co., Ltd. • Founders required: Requires only 2 founders, same as a company registration. Summary: Which one to choose? • Choose a "Company Limited" if you seek the highest credibility, plan to expand the business or branches, want to limit risks to personal assets, have foreign shareholders, or plan to hire foreign employees (Work Permit). • Choose a "Limited Partnership" if it is a family business or close friends you trust 100%, the business has low debt risk, no foreign partners, and you prefer the simplest management structure. Note: Currently, the costs of registration, monthly accounting, and annual financial closing for Co., Ltd. and Ltd. Part. do not differ significantly. Registering as a "Company Limited" generally yields maximum long-term benefits.

items.question: How many days does the processing take?
items.answer: It typically takes about 1-5 working days (excluding public holidays) after receiving complete information and documents from the customer. The timeframe may depend on the queue at the Department of Business Development at that time, as well as the customer's promptness in online identity verification (or OTP submission).

items.question: What is registered capital? Can I register a company if I don't have the full amount?
items.answer: Simply put, "Registered Capital" is the working capital used for business operations. "Paying up the registered capital" does not mean paying someone else; it means depositing funds into the company's bank account (which can be opened after the company is registered). • If you don't have the full lump sum: You can gradually deposit the money. For example, if the registered capital is 1 million THB (minimum 25% paid-up is 250,000 THB), you can start by opening the account with 1,000 THB. When there are business expenses, you can deposit money into the company account and pay it out. These funds do not need to sit idle in the account; they can be used as working capital. • Caution: Except in cases where the company has Foreign Shareholders or is a fully Thai-owned company with registered capital exceeding 5 million THB. In these cases, the law requires actual funds and a Bank Certificate to be shown during the registration process.

items.question: Can foreigners be shareholders, and what financial proof is required?
items.answer: Yes, they can, through a general Company Limited registration. If the company is to be a Thai juristic person, Thai nationals must hold at least 51% of the shares. The conditions regarding financial proof and shareholding ratio are as follows: • If there are foreign shareholders (Thais hold 51% or more): Thai partners must present a Bank Certificate corresponding to their share proportion (Foreigners do not need to show funds). For example: 1. To prepare for a Work Permit: Registered capital of 2 million THB (100% paid-up), Thais holding 51% must show at least 1,020,000 THB in deposits. 2. No Work Permit required: Registered capital of 1 million THB (minimum 25% paid-up), Thais holding 51% must show at least 127,500 THB in deposits. • If shareholders are 100% Thai: If the registered capital does not exceed 5,000,000 THB, no financial proof is needed during the setup process (even if shareholders change and foreigners are brought in later). • If foreigners want to hold 100% (or more than 49%): The company can be registered, but upon completion, the company cannot conduct any business until permission is granted through one of the following channels: 1. Apply for a Foreign Business License (FBL). Operations can begin once the license is received. 2. Apply for investment promotion (BOI). Once the BOI certificate is received, you must apply for a Foreign Business Certificate (FBC) before commencing operations.

items.question: If I don't have an office or location yet, can I still register?
items.answer: Yes, you can register. Normally, a commercial building, shophouse, townhouse, townhome, or residential house can be used as the registration address. However, if you do not have these locations or prefer not to use your own, we offer a Virtual Office service in 3 formats to suit your needs: 1. Use as an address for company registration only. 2. Use as an address for company registration + VAT registration. 3. Use as an address for company registration + VAT registration + Employer registration with Social Security.

items.question: If I don't own the house, such as using a rental house or condo, can I register the company there?
items.answer: Yes, but there are additional documents required: • Rental house/space: A "Letter of Consent to use the premises" is required, along with certified copies of the ID card and house registration of the "House Master or Property Owner" to be submitted with the registration. • Condominiums: Although legally permissible, you should check with the condo's juristic person first. Many projects have internal regulations prohibiting the use of residential units as corporate entities or offices to prevent disturbances to other residents. (We have a Virtual Office service to solve this problem for you.)

items.question: If I don't have a landline telephone number (02), can I register a company?
items.answer: Yes, you can. Currently, the Department of Business Development allows entrepreneurs to use mobile phone numbers for registration. Additionally, for emails, you can use general emails (such as @gmail.com or @hotmail.com); it is not necessary to use an email under the company's domain name.

items.question: Once the company is registered, can I start doing business immediately?
items.answer: For most businesses with general operations, once the juristic person registration is complete, you can start business operations immediately. However, there are certain businesses regulated by specific laws that require additional licenses from relevant government agencies before operating legally. For example: • Tourism and Travel: Tour operators, guides, hotels, accommodations (Must apply for a TAT license or relevant agency). • Health and Food: Restaurants, clinics, hospitals, spas, production/import of food, drugs, cosmetics (Must apply for FDA or Ministry of Public Health license). • Industrial: Factories, possession of chemicals or hazardous materials (Must apply for a license from the Department of Industrial Works). • Financial: Credit businesses like Pico Finance (Must apply for a license from the Bank of Thailand or Ministry of Finance). • Others: Recruitment agencies, security services, logistics and transportation, etc. (Must apply from the Department of Employment, Royal Thai Police, Department of Land Transport, etc.) Important Note: Some businesses may be reserved exclusively for Thai nationals. If your company has foreign shareholders exceeding the set limit or foreign authorized directors, you might not be able to obtain that specific license. To save time and money, we recommend studying the requirements or directly inquiring with the regulating agency before initiating the company registration.

items.question: Do I need to register for Value Added Tax (VAT 7%) immediately after company registration?
items.answer: Not necessarily. Company registration and VAT registration are separate processes. A company is only required to register for VAT when its business income exceeds 1.8 million THB per year. However, if your company needs to import/export goods or deal with other B2B corporate partners who require you to issue tax invoices, your company must apply for VAT registration to be able to issue tax invoices to your partners.

items.question: After registering the company, but before starting sales or generating income, do I need to do accounting?
items.answer: Yes, you must. This is a common misconception among new entrepreneurs. By law (Accounting Act B.E. 2543), all juristic entities are required to prepare accounts and submit relevant reports to the DBD and the Revenue Department, "even if there is no income, no expenses, or business operations haven't started in that month or year." Failure to submit by the deadline will result in significant legal fines.

items.question: As a company director, do I need to deduct my salary for Social Security?
items.answer: No. If you are an authorized director and a major shareholder, you are considered an "Employer", not an employee. Therefore, you cannot and do not need to register as an insured person (Section 33) in your own company. However, as soon as the company starts hiring "Full-time employees", the law requires the company to register as an employer and submit Social Security for the employees within 30 days from the date they start working.

article.title: Why disciplined bookkeeping matters for Thai SMEs
article.author: Virintira Insights
article.intro: Strong bookkeeping is more than compliance—it is the engine that powers confident decisions, investor trust, and sustainable growth.

sections.heading: Turn chaos into clarity
content: Without structured bookkeeping, leaders spend their time hunting receipts and reconciling mismatched spreadsheets. By outsourcing to a dedicated accounting team, you gain reliable numbers every morning.
Clean data enables faster approvals, smarter procurement, and transparent vendor management.

sections.heading: Stay ahead of compliance
content: Thai tax regulations evolve regularly. A disciplined monthly close ensures VAT, withholding tax, and corporate filings are submitted accurately and on time.
Virintira keeps you audit ready with documented workflows and digital archives.

sections.heading: Fuel strategic decisions
content: Investors and lenders require trustworthy statements. When your bookkeeping is current, you can raise funds, expand locations, and hire confidently.
Use cash-flow forecasts and profitability analysis to plan next quarter with certainty.

article.conclusion: When your books are in order, you free up headspace to focus on customers and innovation. Virintira keeps finance smooth so you can keep momentum.

items.description: Align bank feeds with sales platforms to catch issues early.

steps.description: Gather invoices, receipts, bank statements, and payroll reports for the period.

steps.title: Post and reconcile
steps.description: Record transactions in your ledger and reconcile bank, cash, and digital wallets.

steps.title: Review adjustments
steps.description: Accrue expenses, recognise revenue, and verify inventory counts.

steps.title: Generate statements
steps.description: Produce P&L, balance sheet, and cash-flow statements, then share with stakeholders.

formSection.heading: Would you prefer us to contact you back?

hero.title: Grow with confidence in Thailand.
hero.subtitle: Virintira combines accounting expertise with modern technology to deliver end-to-end services that are transparent and trustworthy.

mission.p1: Our mission is to be a firm that provides more than general accounting and tax services. We are a "comprehensive business management and technology firm" that supports entrepreneurs in every dimension of their business operations.
mission.p2: We operate on the principles of accuracy, transparency, and placing our clients' best interests first. We strive to be both a trusted thinking partner who understands the nature of your business and a reliable long-term business ally.
mission.heading: Our Mission

story.p1: Our team has over 10 years of experience in business consulting and management, covering corporate registration, licensing, accounting, taxation, financial closing, and relevant business laws in Thailand.
story.p2: Working alongside various types of entrepreneurs, we found that a major problem many face is not receiving complete information or straightforward explanations for planning and decision-making. Misunderstanding legal details or government agency conditions can lead to severe consequences, such as license revocation, fines, or unnecessary tax burdens.
story.p3: VIRINTIRA was therefore founded with the intention of being a consultant who "speaks the truth." We are ready to lay out all options, explaining the pros, cons, and risks straightforwardly, empowering you to move forward and choose what is best for your business yourself.
story.heading: Our Story

transparency.title: Transparency First
transparency.description: No hidden fees, no opaque processes. We believe in clear communication at every stage.

speed.title: Uncompromising Speed
speed.description: Time is money. Our digital-first approach ensures rapid turnarounds for all filings and reports.

reliability.title: Rock-Solid Reliability
reliability.description: Fully certified by the DBD, we've never left a project unfinished. Your business is safe with us.

cta.heading: Take the next step with confidence together with Virintira.
cta.description: Let our team handle your paperwork and back-office tasks, so you can focus fully on your business goals.

coreValues.card1: Integrity and Transparency: We provide complete information on all options, conceal nothing, and do not persuade clients to use unnecessary services.
coreValues.card2: Comprehensive Expertise: Our services cover accounting, taxation, business law, and technology to perfectly elevate your business.
coreValues.card3: Professional Responsibility: We take full responsibility for assigned tasks, never abandon projects, and deliver work on time (except in cases of delays caused by government agency procedures).
coreValues.card4: Openness and Sincerity: We work with heart, prioritizing our clients' benefits, based on mutual respect and strict adherence to professional standards.
coreValues.heading: Our Core Values

policies.subHeader: To maintain the highest standards and efficiency in our services, we have clear guidelines and policies as follows:
policies.card1: Assessment and Consultation: When clients contact us regarding a service, we thoroughly explain what needs to be prepared, working methods, scope of work, steps, timeline, and service fees. This provides clients with a complete overview to facilitate goal planning.
policies.card2: Payment of Service Fees: To ensure smooth resource allocation and operations, our services require full payment prior to the commencement of work. (For certain services requiring an operational period of more than 1 month, payment can be divided into 2-3 installments, subject to agreed terms and rates.)
policies.card3: Non-Refund Policy: As our service model entails in-depth consulting and professional work—requiring the company to immediately allocate time, resources, and expert personnel to prepare for operations once an agreement is made—the company reserves the right to strictly enforce a no-refund policy in all cases.
policies.heading: Our Service Policies

ethics.p1: At VIRINTIRA, we believe our credibility, performance, and success are not measured by working photos, client photos, or showcasing client lists on public media.
ethics.p2: We have a strict policy against publishing client information, portfolios, or reviews, as we consider your privacy and business confidentiality to be of the utmost importance. Our success is built on the word-of-mouth trust bestowed by our clients and 100% legally compliant work results.
ethics.heading: Ethics and Privacy
